    Past Incidents

    May 16, 2026

    No incidents reported.

    May 11, 2026
    activity log for US instance is delayed
    Resolved - This incident has been resolved.
    May 11, 09:37 PDT
    Update - Activity Log recovered at 07:00 UTC-07:00 today (about 6.5h ago).  All streams are caught up.
    May 8, 13:46 PDT
    Update - All activity log streams are now caught up, except for the query runs table, which should have all data since the start of the incident backfilled by 1600 PDT.
    May 8, 03:40 PDT
    Update - We are continuing to monitor for any further issues.
    May 8, 03:22 PDT
    Update - We believe that the activity log should now be caught up.
    May 8, 03:21 PDT
    Monitoring - Backfilling of the past 2h30m of data is now in progress and should complete shortly.
    May 8, 03:13 PDT
    Identified - Activity log delay is rising, and is more than an hour behind due to a MySQL replica failover. Estimated recovery by 04:00 PDT.
    May 8, 01:00 PDT
    May 10, 2026

    No incidents reported.

    May 9, 2026

    No incidents reported.

    May 8, 2026
    May 7, 2026
    Scheduled Maintenance - US Region
    Completed - Scheduled maintenance is complete. All services are healthy and stable. Ingest, querying SLO processing, anomaly detection, Service Maps, and Activity Log are all confirmed operating normally. Backfill and SLO repair are complete. Thank you for your patience.
    May 7, 15:44 PDT
    Update - We are now in our final verification stage. Query results should begin returning fresh data now.
    May 7, 15:27 PDT
    Verifying - All consumer services have been successfully cut over. SLO backfill is currently running. Anomaly detection, Service Maps, and Activity Log processing are all on the new cluster and catching up. All data will be fully present within approximately 2 hours of the maintenance window closing.
    May 7, 15:13 PDT
    Update - Scheduled maintenance is still in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.
    May 7, 13:53 PDT
    In progress - We have begun scheduled maintenance on our Kafka infrastructure in Production US. During this window, event ingestion and query results may experience delays.
    May 7, 13:10 PDT
    Scheduled - We will be performing scheduled maintenance on our Kafka infrastructure in the US region. This maintenance will only impact the US cluster.

    During this window, you may experience the following:

    Ingest will remain uninterrupted - events will continue to be accepted, although their processing will be delayed. You may notice a gap in your data during the migration window; this data will be backfilled after the migration completes. Expect all data to be present within approximately 3 hours of the maintenance window closing.

    Query results will reflect a delay in fresh data during the cutover window, in line with the ingestion delays.

    Triggers and alerts: Triggers watching for missing events (e.g. heartbeats) will fire during this window. Triggers watching for values above a threshold are not expected to fire. Other alerting sources will process delayed events late, up to approximately 3 hours.

    SLO burn alerts will also fire late during this window.

    Activity Log and Service Maps will experience delays in processing events.

    We appreciate your patience and will provide updates as the maintenance progresses
    Apr 28, 11:49 PDT
    May 6, 2026

    No incidents reported.

    May 5, 2026

    No incidents reported.

    May 4, 2026
    Scheduled Maintenance – EU Region
    Completed - Scheduled maintenance is complete. All services are healthy and stable. Ingest, Querying, SLO processing, Anomaly Detection, Service Maps, Triggers and Activity Log are all confirmed operating normally. Backfill and SLA repair are complete. Thank you for your patience.
    May 4, 13:00 PDT
    Verifying - We are in our final verification phase. Query results, SLO calculations, anomaly detection, and Service Maps are all confirmed processing as expected.
    May 4, 12:08 PDT
    Update - Query results should begin returning fresh data shortly. SLO processing and Service Maps migration are still in progress.
    May 4, 11:14 PDT
    Update - We have begun scheduled maintenance on our Kafka infrastructure in Production EU.
    May 4, 10:28 PDT
    In progress - Scheduled maintenance is currently in progress. We will provide updates as necessary.
    May 4, 10:00 PDT
    Scheduled - We will be performing scheduled maintenance on our Kafka infrastructure in the EU region. This maintenance will only impact the EU cluster.

    During this window, you may experience the following:
    * Ingest will remain uninterrupted - events will continue to be accepted, although their processing will be delayed. You may notice a gap in your data during the migration window; this data will be backfilled after the migration completes. Expect all data to be present within approximately 3 hours of the maintenance window closing.
    * Query results will reflect a delay in fresh data during the cutover window, in line with the ingestion delays.
    * Triggers and alerts: Triggers watching for missing events (e.g. heartbeats) will fire during this window. Triggers watching for values above a threshold are not expected to fire. Other alerting sources will process delayed events late, up to approximately 3 hours.
    * SLO burn alerts will also fire late during this window.
    * Activity Log and Service Maps will experience delays in processing events.

    We appreciate your patience and will provide updates as the maintenance progresses
    Apr 27, 13:58 PDT
